title: aws-ssm: ParameterVersionNotFound wrongly mapped to NotFound
description: Trigger: SSM parameter exists but requested version is deleted/expired. ParameterVersionNotFound is not the same as NotFound — the parameter exists, a version is gone. Mapping both to SecretError::NotFound causes consumer to think the parameter doesn't exist, potentially creating a duplicate or discarding a valid cached value. SecretError::Backend is more accurate. This is a semantic lie in the public API.
